enforced.oracle: After toggling RNEXT between the explicit RNAME and the '=' alias, the parsed SAM object must deep-equal the original. The SAM spec explicitly allows '=' as a synonym for 'same as RNAME'.

evidence.quote: This field is set as '*' when the information is unavailable, and set as '=' if RNEXT is identical RNAME.

enforced.mr_name: CIGAR-SEQ length mismatch must be rejected
enforced.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT (raise validation error or crash) on the malformed record; silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: If not a `*', the length of the sequence must equal the sum of lengths of MIS=X operations in CIGAR.
Sum of lengths of the MIS=X operations shall equal the length of SEQ.

enforced.mr_name: SAM RNEXT '=' alias canonicalization round-trip invariance
enforced.oracle: parse(x) == parse(T(x)) — RNEXT '=' is a spec-authorized synonym for 'same as RNAME' per SAMv1 §1.4.7; the canonical normalizer already handles the explicit side, and this transform exercises the alias side so all parsers' dual-form handling is tested

evidence.quote: This field is set as `*' when the information is unavailable, and set as `=' if RNEXT is identical RNAME.

enforced.mr_name: SAM unmapped mate flags strip round-trip invariance
enforced.oracle: parse(x) == parse(T(x)) — when FLAG bit 0x1 is unset, mate-context bits 0x2, 0x8, 0x20, 0x40, 0x80 have no defined meaning per SAMv1 §1.4; stripping them is semantics-preserving

evidence.quote: If 0x1 is unset, no assumptions can be made about 0x2, 0x8, 0x20, 0x40 and 0x80.

enforced.mr_name: RNEXT '=' alias query invariance under canonicalize_rnext_equals_alias
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method: getMateReferenceName, getReadPairedFlag, getProperPairFlag, getMateUnmappedFlag

evidence.quote: This field is set as `*' when the information is unavailable, and set as `=' if RNEXT is identical RNAME.

enforced.mr_name: SEQ case normalization query invariance under normalize_seq_case
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method: getReadLength, getReadString, getBaseQualityString, getCigarLength

evidence.quote: No assumptions can be made on the letter cases.

enforced.mr_name: Optional tag field permutation query invariance under permute_optional_tag_fields
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method: getReadName, getFlags, getAlignmentStart, getMappingQuality, getCigarString

evidence.quote: Each alignment line has 11 mandatory fields for essential alignment information such as mapping position, and variable number of optional fields for flexible or aligner specific information.

enforced.mr_name: Header record reordering query invariance under reorder_header_records
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method: getReferenceName, getContig, getMateReferenceName, getMateReferenceIndex

evidence.quote: If @SQ header lines are present, RNAME (if not `*') must be present in one of the SQ-SN tag.

enforced.mr_name: Unmapped record normalization query invariance under normalize_unmapped_record_fields
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method: getReadUnmappedFlag, getMappingQuality, getFlags, getDuplicateReadFlag

evidence.quote: Bit 0x4 is the only reliable place to tell whether the read is unmapped. If 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, and bits 0x2, 0x100, and 0x800.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM header subtag order invariance within @HD lines
quarantine.oracle: Parsing the original and shuffled @HD line produces identical structured data; the canonical normalizer sorts subtags so the round-trip is deterministic.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM header subtag order invariance within @SQ lines
quarantine.oracle: Parsing the original and shuffled @SQ lines produces identical structured data; the canonical normalizer sorts subtags so the round-trip is deterministic.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM header subtag order invariance within @RG lines
quarantine.oracle: Parsing the original and shuffled @RG lines produces identical structured data; the canonical normalizer sorts subtags so the round-trip is deterministic.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM header subtag order invariance within @PG lines
quarantine.oracle: Parsing the original and shuffled @PG lines produces identical structured data; the canonical normalizer sorts subtags so the round-trip is deterministic.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM @CO comment line order invariance
quarantine.oracle: Parsing the original and shuffled @CO lines produces identical structured data; @CO lines are free-text comments with no ordering semantics.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

ambiguity_flags: @CO lines are explicitly excluded from the TAG:VALUE format, but the spec does not explicitly state that @CO line order is insignificant — it is implied by their free-text comment nature and the fact that they lack structured tags.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM optional tag field order invariance on alignment records
quarantine.oracle: Parsing the original and permuted alignment records produces identical structured data; the canonical normalizer sorts optional tags so the round-trip is deterministic.

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM @SQ / @RG / @PG header record order invariance
quarantine.oracle: Parsing the original and reordered header produces identical structured data; only @HD must remain first.

evidence.quote: @HD & File-level metadata. Optional. If present, there must be only one @HD line and it must be the first line of the file.

ambiguity_flags: The spec mandates @HD first but does not mandate any specific order among @SQ, @RG, @PG lines — their relative order is implicitly unconstrained.

quarantine.mr_name: SEQ case normalization invariance
quarantine.oracle: After uppercasing the SEQ field, the parsed SAM object must deep-equal the original. The SAM spec permits both upper and lower case in SEQ.

evidence.quote: Sum of lengths of the MIS=X operations shall equal the length of SEQ.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly state that case is insignificant in SEQ, but the regex permits both upper and lower case letters. Some parsers may treat case differently.

quarantine.mr_name: CIGAR zero-length op removal invariance
quarantine.oracle: After stripping zero-length CIGAR ops and merging adjacent identical ops, the parsed SAM object must deep-equal the original. The SAM Recommended Practice states adjacent CIGAR operations should be different.

evidence.quote: Sum of lengths of the MIS=X operations shall equal the length of SEQ.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly forbid zero-length CIGAR ops, only the Recommended Practice advises against adjacent identical ops.

quarantine.mr_name: Unmapped record MAPQ normalization invariance
quarantine.oracle: After normalizing MAPQ to 255 for unmapped reads (FLAG bit 0x4 set), the parsed SAM object must deep-equal the original. MAPQ is not meaningful for unmapped reads.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p137
evidence.quote: If all segments in a template are unmapped, their RNAME should be set as '*' and POS as 0.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec says MAPQ 255 means 'not available' but does not mandate it for unmapped reads specifically; this is a Picard validation rule.

quarantine.mr_name: Mate flag stripping for unpaired reads invariance
quarantine.oracle: When FLAG bit 0x1 (multiple segments) is unset, clearing mate-context bits (0x2, 0x8, 0x20, 0x40, 0x80) must produce a deep-equal parsed object. These bits are only meaningful when 0x1 is set per SAMv1 §1.4.

evidence.quote: RNEXT: Reference sequence name of the primary alignment of the NEXT read in the template.

ambiguity_flags: The spec defines these bits' meanings when 0x1 is set but does not explicitly state they must be zero when 0x1 is unset; this is a Picard validation rule.

quarantine.mr_name: CIGAR split/merge normalization invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P extracts CIGAR string, alignment start, alignment end, and read length; splitting 10M into 4M6M or merging 4M6M into 10M preserves the total query-consumed length and reference-consumed length

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p134
evidence.quote: [SAM v1 — Recommended Practice for the SAM Format] * Adjacent CIGAR operations should be different.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec says 'should be different' (ADVISORY), not 'must be different' (CRITICAL), so adjacent identical ops are technically valid but not recommended

quarantine.mr_name: Flag 0x4 set on mapped read with valid RNAME/POS must be rejected
quarantine.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT (raise validation error or crash) when FLAG 0x4 is set but RNAME is not '*' and POS > 0; silent acceptance is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: Bit 0x4 is the only reliable place to tell whether the read is unmapped. If 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, and bits 0x2, 0x100, and 0x800.
An unmapped segment without coordinate has a `*' at this field. However, an unmapped segment may also have an ordinary coordinate such that it can be placed at a desired position after sorting.

ambiguity_flags: The spec says 'no assumptions can be made' rather than explicitly stating 'MUST reject', but the contradiction of setting 0x4 on a mapped read with valid RNAME/POS is a well-known validation error (Picard INVALID_FLAG_MAPPED_UNMAPPED)

quarantine.mr_name: Illegal optional tag type character must be rejected
quarantine.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT (raise validation error or crash) when an optional tag's type character is not one of A, i, f, Z, H, B; silent acceptance is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: As with alignment optional fields (see Section~sec:alnaux), you can freely add new tags for further data fields.

ambiguity_flags: The spec reference to alignment optional fields (sec:alnaux) defines the type encoding table (A, i, f, Z, H, B) but the chunk retrieved does not contain the full table; the rule is well-established in SAMtags §2.1

quarantine.mr_name: TLEN sign consistency violation for paired reads must be rejected
quarantine.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT (raise validation error or crash) when both reads of a paired template carry same-signed TLEN values; silent acceptance is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: The TLEN field is positive for the leftmost segment of the template, negative for the rightmost, and the sign for any middle segment is undefined. If segments cover the same coordinates then the choice of which is leftmost and rightmost is arbitrary, but the two ends must still have differing signs.
The TLEN field is positive for the leftmost segment of the template, negative for the rightmost... but the two ends must still have differing signs.

ambiguity_flags: The spec says 'must still have differing signs' for the two ends, but does not explicitly state what a parser should do when this rule is violated; however, the normative 'must' language implies invalid data

quarantine.mr_name: POS shift with SQ LN bound check preserves alignment end coordinate
quarantine.oracle: After shifting POS by +shift and increasing @SQ LN by +shift, the alignment end coordinate (POS + sum of ref-consuming CIGAR ops - 1) remains within the new LN boundary, and the relative ordering of alignments by coordinate is preserved.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p138
evidence.quote: If POS plus the sum of lengths of M=XDN operations in CIGAR exceeds the length specified in the LN field of the @SQ header line (if exists) with an SN equal to RNAME, the alignment should be unmapped, unless the reference sequence is circular (see below).
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

evidence.quote: PNEXT: 1-based Position of the primary alignment of the NEXT read in the template. Set as 0 when the information is unavailable.

ambiguity_flags: The spec says 'should be unmapped' (ADVISORY) rather than 'must' — some implementations may accept overhanging alignments while others reject them

quarantine.mr_name: SAM-BAM round-trip preserves 1-based POS coordinate system
quarantine.oracle: After round-tripping through BAM (which uses 0-based coordinates internally), the POS field in the output SAM must be identical to the input POS, confirming correct 1-based↔0-based coordinate conversion in the BAM codec.

evidence.quote: 1-based coordinate system A coordinate system where the first base of a sequence is one. In this coordinate system, a region is specified by a closed interval. For example, the region between the 3rd and the 7th bases inclusive is [3,7]. The SAM, VCF, GFF and Wiggle formats are using the 1-based coordinate system.

0-based coordinate system A coordinate system where the first base of a sequence is zero. In this coordinate system, a region is specified by a half-closed-half-open interval. For example, the region between the 3rd and the 7th bases inclusive is [2,7). The BAM, BCFv2, BED, and PSL formats are using the 0-based coordinate system.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM-CRAM round-trip preserves POS and alignment end coordinate
quarantine.oracle: After round-tripping through CRAM, the POS field and alignment end coordinate (POS + ref_consume - 1) must be preserved, confirming correct coordinate handling in the CRAM codec despite potential lossy compression of CIGAR (=/X collapsed to M).

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p138
evidence.quote: If POS plus the sum of lengths of M=XDN operations in CIGAR exceeds the length specified in the LN field of the @SQ header line (if exists) with an SN equal to RNAME, the alignment should be unmapped, unless the reference sequence is circular (see below).
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

evidence.quote: All mapped segments in alignment lines are represented on the forward genomic strand.

ambiguity_flags: CRAM may collapse =/X CIGAR ops to M, which changes CIGAR representation but not the reference span — the oracle must account for this via cram_safe canonicalization

quarantine.mr_name: Query method invariance under header record reordering for coordinate queries
quarantine.oracle: After reordering @SQ or @RG header lines, query methods that return coordinate information (getAlignmentStart, getAlignmentEnd, getStart, getEnd, getLengthOnReference) must return identical values since the alignment records themselves are unchanged.

evidence.quote: PNEXT: 1-based Position of the primary alignment of the NEXT read in the template. Set as 0 when the information is unavailable.

The alignment section: mandatory fields... Each line consists of 11 or more TAB-separated fields. The first eleven fields are always present and in the order shown below; if the information represented by any of these fields is unavailable, that field's value will be a placeholder, either `0' or `*' as determined by the field's type.

ambiguity_flags: Reordering @SQ lines changes the reference index mapping — getReferenceIndex() may change even though getReferenceName() stays the same

quarantine.mr_name: SAM header subtag shuffle round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: parse(x) == parse(T(x)) — shuffling @HD subtag order is semantics-preserving per SAMv1 §1.3; the canonical normalizer sorts subtags so the oracle passes deterministically

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM @SQ record subtag shuffle round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: parse(x) == parse(T(x)) — shuffling @SQ subtag order is semantics-preserving per SAMv1 §1.3; the canonical normalizer sorts subtags so the oracle passes deterministically

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM @PG record subtag shuffle round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: parse(x) == parse(T(x)) — shuffling @PG subtag order is semantics-preserving per SAMv1 §1.3; the canonical normalizer sorts subtags so the oracle passes deterministically

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM @RG record subtag shuffle round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: parse(x) == parse(T(x)) — shuffling @RG subtag order is semantics-preserving per SAMv1 §1.3; the canonical normalizer sorts subtags so the oracle passes deterministically

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM @CO comment line shuffle round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: parse(x) == parse(T(x)) — @CO comment lines have no ordering semantics per SAMv1 §1.3; the canonical normalizer sorts them so the oracle passes deterministically

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM optional tag field permutation round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: parse(x) == parse(T(x)) — optional field order is not significant per SAMv1 §1.5; the canonical normalizer sorts them so the oracle passes deterministically

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM BAM codec round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: parse(x) == parse(T(x)) — BAM is the binary equivalent of SAM per SAMv1 §4; a round-trip through the BAM codec must preserve all information content

evidence.quote: Optional alignment fields are stored immediately after each other immediately following the qual field, and are included in block_size.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM CIGAR zero-length op removal round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: parse(x) == parse(T(x)) — zero-length CIGAR ops carry no alignment information; removing them and merging adjacent same-type ops is semantics-preserving per SAM Recommended Practice

evidence.quote: Sum of lengths of the MIS=X operations shall equal the length of SEQ.

ambiguity_flags: SAMv1 §1.4.6 does not explicitly forbid zero-length ops, but the Recommended Practice (p134) says adjacent CIGAR operations should be different

quarantine.mr_name: SAM SEQ case normalization round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: parse(x) == parse(T(x)) — SEQ letter case is not semantically significant per SAMv1 §1.4.10; uppercasing is a semantics-preserving normalization

evidence.quote: The case-insensitive base codes `=ACMGRSVTWYHKDBN' are mapped to [0,15] respectively with all other characters mapping to `N' (value 15).

ambiguity_flags: The spec regex permits both upper and lower case in SEQ, but BAM encoding treats bases case-insensitively

quarantine.mr_name: SAM CIGAR split-or-merge adjacent ops round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: parse(x) == parse(T(x)) — splitting or merging adjacent identical CIGAR ops preserves total query-consumed length and alignment semantics

evidence.quote: Sum of lengths of the MIS=X operations shall equal the length of SEQ.

ambiguity_flags: SAMv1 §1.4.6 does not mandate that adjacent CIGAR ops be of different types, though the Recommended Practice suggests it

quarantine.mr_name: SAM header record reorder round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: parse(x) == parse(T(x)) — reordering @SQ or @RG lines while keeping @HD first is semantics-preserving; the canonical normalizer sorts header records so the oracle passes deterministically

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM HD VN version bump round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: parse(x) == parse(T(x)) — toggling @HD VN between 1.5 and 1.6 is backwards-compatible per SAM version history; no 1.6-only features are used in the test corpus

evidence.quote: Each header line begins with the character `@' followed by one of the two-letter header record type codes defined in this section.

ambiguity_flags: SAM v1.5→1.6 added clarifications but no mandatory new fields; some SUTs may version-gate parser behavior on VN

quarantine.mr_name: CIGAR zero-length op removal query invariance under cigar_zero_length_op_removal
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method: getCigarLength, getCigarString, getLengthOnReference, getAlignmentEnd, getAlignmentStart

evidence.quote: Sum of lengths of the MIS=X operations shall equal the length of SEQ.

ambiguity_flags: SAMv1 §1.4.6 doesn't forbid 0-length ops but the SAM Recommended Practice (p134) says 'adjacent CIGAR operations should be different'
